meaning: n.缓刑(尤指缓期执行死刑),缓刑令,(痛苦,烦恼等的)暂缓vt.暂减,缓期执行
examples: It looked as though the college would have to shut, but this week it was given a reprieve.
Campaigners have won a reprieve for the hospital threatened with closure.
The cost of the reprieve was high, however.
The icy water is refreshing in the heat, and provides a reprieve from mosquitoes.
Because Greece was small enough to bail out, they have bought a temporary reprieve.
